title: montia parviflora var. depressa
intro: Montia parviflora var. depressa, also known as 'spleenwort' or 'pennywort', is a type of flowering plant in the family Portulacaceae, characterized by its small, rounded leaves and yellow-green flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Montia parviflora var. depressa include its ability to support liver and kidney function, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
